message:
test(readme): cover 5 missing _df_fill arithmetic variants (fd90.227 / br-yzsks)

Extends readme_element_wise_operations_compiles_and_runs to cover the
5 _df_fill arithmetic variants that previously had no coverage:

- sub_df_fill, mul_df_fill, div_df_fill, floordiv_df_fill, mod_df_fill

With fd90.226 + fd90.227 combined, the full DataFrame arithmetic
matrix is now exercised:
- 7 _scalar (add/sub/mul/div/pow/mod/floordiv)
- 7 _df (same 7)
- 6 _df_fill (no pow_df_fill in fp-frame)

Verified: 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -937,6 +937,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-yvkrb","title":"fd90.145: remove dead FrameError::DuplicateIndexUnsupported variant (followup to fd90.140 docs cleanup)","description":"fd90.74-144: follow-on to fd90.140. The FrameError::DuplicateIndexUnsupported variant declared at crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:113 is dead code — declared but never constructed anywhere in the workspace:

  grep -rnE 'DuplicateIndexUnsupported' crates/
  → only the declaration; zero call-site constructions

fd90.140 already removed it from the README's Error Architecture row. Now do the corresponding code cleanup. The enum is marked '#[non_exhaustive]' (line 108), so removing a variant is SemVer-safe — downstream code can't construct or exhaustively match the enum without acknowledging future additions/removals.

The error message ('duplicate index labels are unsupported in strict mode for MVP slice') reads like reserved-for-future-MVP language. If/when strict-mode duplicate handling lands, the variant can be re-added with proper construction sites — at that point the README listing will also update.

Fix: remove the variant + its #[error] attribute. cargo build -p fp-frame and cargo test -p fp-frame --lib should both pass with no changes since nothing references it.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T13:49:29.487955759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:51:20.033526080Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:51:20.030804620Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Land any divergence as [HIGH] review bead with failing input + actual + expected.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T03:21:58.270794844Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T05:21:08.841665809Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T05:21:08.839830916Z","close_reason":"Added 9 live-oracle GroupBy parity matrix cases","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","groupby","parity-matrix"]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-yzsks","title":"fd90.227: Cover 5 missing _df_fill arithmetic methods","description":"fd90.227: Cover 5 missing _df_fill arithmetic methods.

DataFrame has 6 *_df_fill methods (add/sub/mul/div/floordiv/mod) at fp-frame:24604-24647 — they take a fill_value f64 in addition to the operand DataFrame. Only add_df_fill is integration-tested. The other 5 (sub_df_fill, mul_df_fill, div_df_fill, floordiv_df_fill, mod_df_fill) have no coverage.

Scope:
- Extend readme_element_wise_operations_compiles_and_runs with 5 missing _df_fill calls.

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs.
Time estimate: 5-10min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-27T03:20:10.187805239Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T03:21:58.615353539Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T03:21:58.613143985Z","close_reason":"Extended readme_element_wise_operations_compiles_and_runs to cover the 5 _df_fill arithmetic variants that previously had no coverage: sub_df_fill, mul_df_fill, div_df_fill, floordiv_df_fill, mod_df_fill. With fd90.226 + fd90.227 combined, all 6 _df_fill methods + all 7 _df + all 7 _scalar arithmetic methods are integration-tested. All 35 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-z1615","title":"fd90.160: add Financial Data Pipeline integration test (read_csv + groupby.agg_named + write_jsonl)","description":"fd90.74-159 follow-on. The README's 'Financial Data Pipeline' recipe (lines 1305-1333) is the last major Recipes section without integration test coverage. fd90.146 covers Quick Example + Quick Start; fd90.156 / fd90.157 / fd90.158 / fd90.159 cover Merge: Advanced Options / Expression-Driven Analysis / MultiIndex Operations / Categorical Analysis. The Financial Data Pipeline exercises a multi-stage chain that's not covered elsewhere:
  - read_csv_str with multi-line input
  - Series::new(name, Index, Column) constructor
  - df.column(name).unwrap().clone() to extract Column
  - to_datetime on a Utf8 series of ISO dates
  - df.groupby(&[col])?.agg_named(&[(out, src, fn), ...])? multi-aggregation
  - write_jsonl with a tempfile path

Fix: add 'readme_financial_data_pipeline_compiles_and_runs' which mirrors the example. Use a tempdir from std for the JSONL output path so the test cleans up after itself. Verify the agg_named output has the expected per-ticker columns + write succeeds.

Verify with cargo test -p frankenpandas. Pure additive test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T15:37:43.286638038Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T15:40:02.926877890Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T15:40:02.924246016Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-z2o49","title":"fd90.148: README FAQ Column memory claim wrong — actual storage is Vec<Scalar>, not typed arrays","description":"fd90.74-147 README audit. FAQ at line 1507 claims:
  'Column stores typed arrays (Vec<f64>, Vec<i64>) instead of object arrays.'

But the actual Column struct at crates/fp-columnar/src/lib.rs is:
  pub struct Column {
      dtype: DType,
      values: Vec<Scalar>,  // <-- Vec<Scalar>, NOT Vec<f64>/Vec<i64>
      validity: ValidityMask,
  }

Column persistently stores Vec<Scalar> (a tagged enum per element). The typed-array form is ColumnData (line 327), which is MATERIALIZED ON-DEMAND for AG-10 vectorized arithmetic and then thrown away — it's a transient view, not the storage layout.

Pandas (via numpy) stores native primitive arrays (f64 / i64 / etc.) so each cell is just sizeof(T) bytes. FrankenPandas stores Vec<Scalar> where each cell is sizeof(Scalar) — which is substantially larger than sizeof(f64) due to the enum discriminant + the largest variant (Utf8(String) = 24 bytes minimum).

So the README's:
- 'Column stores typed arrays (Vec<f64>, Vec<i64>)' — wrong; actual storage is Vec<Scalar>
- 'Comparable for numeric data' — misleading; FrankenPandas Column is HIGHER memory overhead than pandas numpy arrays for numeric data

Fix: rewrite the FAQ Q answer to honestly describe the dual storage:
- Persistent storage: Vec<Scalar> (tagged enum, larger per-cell than pandas)
- Vectorized batch path: ColumnData materializes Vec<f64> / Vec<i64> on demand (AG-10) for SIMD-auto-vectorizable arithmetic, then drops back to Vec<Scalar>

Pure README change. No code impact.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T14:06:44.919925528Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T14:08:07.068947541Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T14:08:07.066042399Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
